arts.ttl: Hoffenheim v Tottenham Hotspur: Europa League – live
arts.sum: Europa League updates from the 5.45pm GMT kick-off Live scores | Tables | Read Football Daily | Email Simon At this moment, when they are wrecked by self-doubt and stink of the foul funk of failure, the footballing fates have handed Spurs the precious gift of encouraging fixtures. Sure, they played the first of those, against an Everton team that had scored one goal in their previous six league games, on Sunday and managed to make their opponents look like the 1970 Brazil side, concede three and lose, but they have a chance to make up for it (kind of) tonight, and another against Leicester this weekend. Their season can still come good (or, at least, better). Hoffenheim, 15th in the Bundesliga, three points above 16th-placed Heidenheim and the threat of a relegation play-off, have lost six and won just one of their last 10 games in all competitions, that solitary victory coming against 17th-placed Holstein Kiel on Saturday. “I play football because I want to win, and when you don’t win for so long, it really gets you down,” said the defender Kevin Akpoguma. “In the changing room after the game, you could feel that a weight had been lifted. After a win, everything is just nicer. A win always energises you, that’s been noticeable in the past few days too. But we can’t ease off now, we need to keep it up. It’s important that we all develop a hunger for it, so that we have this feeling much more often.” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Ex-Tottenham star who called Dele Alli decision 'pathetic' clashed with Cesc Fabregas
arts.sum: Despite a previous spat that saw the now Como manager taunt Tottenham over their trophy cabinet, both figures are united in their support for the former Spurs star, who has suffered difficult times in his personal life away from the pitch. In defence of the midfielder during his struggles at Spurs under Mourinho in the 2020/21 campaign, Roberts did not hold back, saying after the Portuguese boss' dismissal: "I've always liked Dele Alli, I think he's a great player.

arts.ttl: Tottenham drop Mikey Moore team news hint for Hoffenheim as Ange Postecoglou decision made
arts.sum: Ange Postecoglou is only facing a handful of Tottenham team selection decisions for the Europa League clash against Hoffenheim with injury issues meaning at least five players are almost guaranteed to start. The Spurs striker has picked up a knee injury that is expected to keep him out for a number of weeks while Pape Matar Sarr appeared to be in discomfort during Tottenham's open training session on Wednesday .

arts.ttl: Tottenham guaranteed to be without 14 players for Hoffenheim Europa League clash
arts.sum: Unavailable players: Kinsky, Vicario, Romero, Van de Ven, Udogie, Spence, Reguilon, Bissouma, Sarr, Johnson, Odobert, Werner, Yang, Solanke. Guglielmo Vicario, Destiny Udogie, Ben Davies, Micky van de Ven, Cristian Romero, Wilson Odobert and Richarlison have spent much of that tie on the sidelines, while Rodrigo Bentancur has spent a lengthy period suspended, while he has been out for two weeks now injured too.
